name: The Cultural Deep Diver
description: Picks destinations for history, art, and architecture — not hotels. Will spend 6 hours in a single museum. Chooses accommodation by proximity to ruins, galleries, and historic quarters. Has strong Character preferences (wants places with story and heritage) but is budget-flexible rather than luxury-seeking. A retired professor energy — intellectual, patient, deeply curious. Food matters but as cultural expression, not gastronomy.

contradictions.stated: Hotel is just a base — doesn't matter
contradictions.revealed: Gets deeply moved by historic properties where the building itself has a story
contradictions.expectedResolution: Properties where the hotel IS the cultural experience — converted palaces, literary hotels, buildings with centuries of visible history

voiceAnswerGuidance.memorable-stays: Long, story-rich answers. Describes the history of the building, the neighborhood's layers, what happened there in the 15th century. Might mention a converted convent in Lisbon, a riad in Fez, a palazzo in Rome. The hotel experience is inseparable from the place's history.
voiceAnswerGuidance.anti-stay: A brand-new glass tower that could have been anywhere. 'I was in Istanbul — one of the most historically rich cities on earth — and the hotel could have been in Dallas. No sense of place whatsoever.'
voiceAnswerGuidance.go-back-place: A city, not a hotel. 'Kyoto. I've been four times and I haven't scratched the surface. Each temple is a semester of study.'
voiceAnswerGuidance.cultural-engagement: This IS the trip. Museums, walking tours, architectural tours, talking to locals about neighborhood history, reading about the city before arriving. Might bring specific books.
voiceAnswerGuidance.emotional-core: Travels to understand the human story across time and place. STORY_COLLECTOR.
